news.headline: Access Medical Labs in Jupiter, Florida confirms Elizabeth Holmes' exact Pfizer study results using the same components, reshaping the conversation around Theranos.
news.summary: A recent announcement in Jupiter, Florida on February 28, 2025 has turned heads as one of the biggest Specialty Labs in the Nation revealed that Elizabeth Holmes' diagnostic technology at Theranos was indeed accurate. This revelation is set to transform the discussions surrounding Holmes and her endeavors. The confirmation that her technology was reliable comes as a significant breakthrough in the ongoing scrutiny over her work. The specific numbers regarding the accuracy rates and impact of this revelation were not disclosed.

news.headline: Global Partners LP filed its 2024 annual report on Form 10-K with SEC on Feb 28, 2025. Available on its investor relations website and SEC's website.
news.summary: Global Partners LP (NYSE: GLP) from Waltham, Mass., has recently disclosed its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal period ending on December 31, 2024. This report was officially submitted to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on February 28, 2025. Interested parties can access and download a copy of the Form 10-K from the Partnership’s investor relations website or the SEC’s official website at www.sec.gov. Additionally, a physical copy of the Partnership’s comprehensive audited financial statements can be requested.

news.headline: S&P: The outlook for France's open economy is uncertain due to weak business & household confidence and elevated public & private sector debt.
S&P: By 2028, we forecast France's average cost of debt will roughly equal nominal GDP growth.
S&P: The negative outlook on France reflects rising government debt amid weak political consensus for tackling France's large underlying budget deficits.
Triumph Financial declares $17.81 per share dividend for 7.125% Series C preferred stock, $0.44525 per depositary share.
news.summary: In Dallas on February 28, 2025, Triumph Financial, Inc. (Nasdaq: TFIN) declared a quarterly cash dividend of $17.81 per share on its 7.125% Series C Fixed-Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock. This stock is represented by depositary shares (Nasdaq: TFINP), where each share represents a 1/40th interest in a preferred stock. Holders of these depositary shares will receive $0.44525 per depositary share. The dividend is set to be paid on March 30, 2025, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on March 15, 2025.

news.headline: Mondelēz appoints Norberto Chaclin as EVP, Chief R&D Officer to drive R&D & quality strategy. Norberto reports to CEO Van de Put.
news.summary: On February 28, 2025, Mondelēz International appointed Norberto Chaclin as the new Executive Vice President and Chief Research & Development Officer. Chaclin, reporting to Chair and CEO Dirk Van de Put, will lead the company's R&D and quality strategy to advance its goal of innovating the snacking industry. Mondelēz International, listed on Nasdaq as MDLZ, sees this appointment as pivotal in shaping their future ventures.

news.headline: 4DMT announced full year 2024 financial results: $505 million in cash as of Dec 31. Positive milestones for 4D-150 and 4D-710 products described. Phase 3 trials for 4FRONT-1 and 4FRONT-2 in wet AMD expected to start in March 2025 and Q3 2025, with topline data in 2H 2027. Cash expected to fund operations until 2028. In ophthalmology, 4D-150 showing positive data in wet AMD and DME, while 4D-710 for CF lung disease proceeding in Phase 1 AEROW trial. Revenue and operational highlights noted for 4DMT, enhancing prospects for 2025 and beyond.
news.summary: 4D Molecular Therapeutics reported positive interim data for its 4D-150 drug in wet AMD and DME clinical trials, showcasing robust and durable clinical activity. In the Phase 1/2 trials, 4D-150 demonstrated significant reduction in injection burden and improvement in visual acuity. The company also announced $505 million in cash reserves as of December 31, 2024, which is expected to fund operations into 2028. The Phase 3 program for 4D-150 in wet AMD is on track, with enrollment for 4FRONT-1 expected in March 2025 and for 4FRONT-2 in Q3 2025, with topline data expected in the second half of 2027. The company is strategically focusing its pipeline on advancing 4D-150 for wet AMD and DME, and 4D-710 for cystic fibrosis, aiming to optimize resource allocation and extend cash runway. On the ophthalmology side, the 4D-150 drug showed positive results with improved visual acuity and reduction in injection burden, leading to potential future widespread adoption in clinics. The company also provided updates on other programs and upcoming milestones. The CEO expressed optimism for the company's progress in 2025 and beyond, positioning them for success based on the advancements made in 2024.

news.headline: Casella Waste Systems to offer $25.0 million in bonds through VT Economic Development Authority, for VT projects.
news.summary: Casella Waste Systems, Inc. is offering $25.0 million in Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds to support their capital projects in Vermont. This offering is part of a larger bond issuance, following an initial $35.0 million loan in June 2022. The Bonds are backed by a Guaranty from Casella's subsidiaries, ensuring repayment as per the Loan Agreement. The funding will be utilized for various capital projects within the state. The deadline for drawing down the remainder of the Bonds is June 1, 2025, after which the funds will no longer be available.

news.headline: Stocks and cryptos dip amid tariffs and trade tensions, with Bitcoin down 20% and a $1.5 million security breach at Bybit.
news.summary: Market sentiments turned bearish, leading to a decline in risk assets due to various factors such as escalating trade tensions, unsatisfactory earnings reports, and indications of economic weakness. President Donald Trump announced a 25% tariff on imports from Mexico and Canada starting on March 4, along with plans to impose a 10% tariff on Chinese imports and European Union goods, particularly targeting automobiles. These significant trade actions caused widespread market unease, resulting in a substantial sell-off across various asset classes. Cryptocurrencies, in particular, faced the brunt of this risk aversion, with Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) experiencing a slight recovery after briefly dipping below $80,000 in Friday trading. Despite this recovery, Bitcoin remains in a bear market as it has dropped over 20% from its peak. The digital asset space also witnessed deteriorating sentiment following a major security breach at Bybit, where hackers managed to steal $1.5 million in assets.

news.headline: S&P: France's outlook changed to negative from stable.

news.headline: For the month, the S&P 500 fell 1.43%, the Nasdaq declined 3.97%, and the Dow fell 1.58%.
Dfinity founder says secure onchain UI could have prevented Bybit hack. Decentralized projects should leverage blockchain's security.
news.summary: Dominic Williams emphasizes the need for decentralized projects to move away from centralized infrastructure and harness the robust security benefits offered by blockchain technology. This shift is crucial to ensure the integrity and resilience of projects in a decentralized ecosystem. Williams highlights the importance of leveraging blockchain's security features, including immutability and decentralization, to enhance the overall reliability of decentralized systems. Embracing these aspects can significantly bolster the security posture of projects and mitigate the risks associated with centralized dependencies. It is essential for decentralized initiatives to prioritize the adoption of blockchain's security capabilities to fortify their operational frameworks and safeguard against potential vulnerabilities.

news.headline: FDA approved Odactra for ages 5-11 to treat dust mite allergies. News roundup on Feb 28, 2025 in Silver Spring, Md.
news.summary: In Silver Spring, Maryland, on February 28, 2025, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration issued a summary of recent updates. A notable development includes the FDA's approval of Odactra for use in children aged 5 to 11 to address house dust mite-induced issues. This decision expands the approved age range for this treatment option, providing relief to a wider group of individuals.

news.headline: Amgen to present at 45th Annual TD Cowen Health Care Conference on March 5, 2025 at 9:10am ET. Jay Bradner to participate.
news.summary: In Thousand Oaks, California, on February 28, 2025, Amgen (NASDAQ: AMGN) announced their participation at the 45th Annual TD Cowen Health Care Conference on March 5, 2025, at 9:10 a.m. ET. news.headline: Oil industry hit by rising steel pipe costs due to Trump's tariffs on steel, aluminum imports. Prices surged by [specific numbers].
news.summary: After President Trump announced plans to implement tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, the price of steel pipes for oil and gas well lining increased. The precise percentage of the price hike was not disclosed, but industry experts reported a noticeable surge in cost. This development is likely to impact the oil and gas sector significantly, as companies will have to adjust their budgets to accommodate the new expenses. President Trump's decision is expected to have lasting effects on the steel market and various industries reliant on these materials.

news.headline: Guggenheim lays off 20 workers due to financial struggles, following Brooklyn Museum's 10% staff reduction.
news.summary: The museum experienced an increase in costs and a decrease in attendance, leading to budget cuts. This decision comes after the Brooklyn Museum reduced its staff by 10% earlier this month.

news.headline: Verizon announces quarterly dividend of 67.75 cents per share on February 28.
news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc.'s Board of Directors, listed on NYSE and Nasdaq as VZ, announced a quarterly dividend of 67.75 cents per share.

news.headline: Dallas plastic surgeon Dr. Rod J. Rohrich hosts the 28th Annual Dallas Cosmetic Surgery and Medicine Meeting on Feb 27-28, 2025.
news.summary: A prominent plastic surgeon, Dr. Rod J. Rohrich, is organizing the 2025 Dallas Cosmetic Surgery and Medicine Meeting scheduled for February 27-28, 2025. The event is being held in Dallas, and Dr. Rohrich serves as the Meeting Chair. This annual gathering is a significant platform for professionals in the field, attracting a wide audience.

news.headline: Crypto industry shaken by a $1.4B Bybit hack, "Kill Tether" campaign, and Bitcoin crash this week.
news.summary: In the past week, the crypto market has been hit hard by several major events. A staggering $1.4 billion hack targeted Bybit, causing significant losses. Furthermore, there have been movements to undermine Tether, a popular stablecoin in the industry. Alongside these issues, the market also saw a noticeable drop in Bitcoin's price, adding to the challenges faced by investors and traders.

news.headline: Bitcoin's rally tied to Trump's electoral victory has ended. Traders turn to Fed rate cuts and global liquidity expansion.
news.summary: Following the recent United States election, Bitcoin saw a surge to record highs in anticipation of Donald Trump's trade policies. However, the situation has changed, and traders are now turning their attention to the potential reintroduction of quantitative easing (QE) and the increase in global liquidity to drive market optimism. This shift in focus comes after Bitcoin reached peak levels, highlighting the uncertainty surrounding political events and their impact on financial markets.

news.headline: Billions Network introduces new digital ID system without biometric verification, targets global market.
news.summary: A recent report revealed that the unemployment rate in the United States has risen to 6.1% in the month of June. This marks an increase from the previous month's rate of 5.8%. The economy added 559,000 jobs in June, which is lower than the expected number of 650,000. These numbers indicate a slower-than-anticipated recovery in the labor market. The sectors that saw the most significant job growth included leisure and hospitality, professional and business services, and education and health services. However, employment in retail trade declined during this period. Despite these fluctuations, the overall trend suggests a modest improvement in the job market as the nation continues its economic recovery from the impacts of the pandemic.

news.headline: GSA's certification programs grew 5% in 2024, with 88% retention rate, reaching 4,168 facilities in 45 countries.
news.summary: In Portsmouth, New Hampshire, on February 28, 2025, it was reported that the Global Seafood Alliance's (GSA) certification programs, Best Aquaculture Practices (BAP) and Best Seafood Practices (BSP), experienced a 5 percent growth in 2024. This growth resulted in a total of 4,168 certified facilities across 45 countries by the end of the year. This signifies a significant increase in the adoption of sustainable practices within the seafood industry globally.

news.headline: JPMorgan CEO Dimon: JPM has 450 AI use cases, we're likely to have 1,000 in a year.
